input.headline: When racing thoughts are interrupting your sleep, here’s what to do

raw.headline: When racing thoughts are interrupting your sleep, here’s what to do

winner: Instead of racing thoughts keeping you up at night, perhaps try sleeping during the day when the track is closed for maintenance!

candidates.text: I tried to race my thoughts during the day, but they kept interrupting my sleep! So now I'm training them for the daytime Olympics instead.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Creative twist on the problem, with a surprising and specific solution involving training thoughts for the Olympics."}

candidates.text: Why don't racing thoughts ever win a snooze-athlon? Because they always come in first and can't seem to sleep!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'snooze-athlon,' but the punchline feels a bit forced and lacks refinement. It's not fully surprising or specific enough to be highly effective."}

candidates.text: Instead of racing thoughts keeping you up at night, perhaps try sleeping during the day when the track is closed for maintenance!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and clever wordplay on 'track' and 'maintenance'"}

candidates.text: A man decided to race his thoughts during daylight hours instead, becoming the first person to win a 24-hour marathon against his own sleeplessness.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative but lacks punch and clarity. The concept of racing thoughts during daylight hours is a bit abstract and doesn't fully land due to the awkward phrasing and lack of clear humor payoff."}

candidates.text: A speedy solution: Start slow-paced meditation during daylight hours, ensuring tranquility by nightfall.
